claims.statement: The sector corpus now has 1 decision packets and 1 theses pointing at AI infrastructure, cicadas, and synthetic biology, while the scanner highlights AVGO and TSM and the paper book already tracks 6 open and 62 closed rows.
claims.falsification: Reject the setup if packet invalidations fire, scanner asymmetry disappears, or the book exposure already prices the obvious expression.
claims.object_title: AI infrastructure: The Scanner Versus the Corpus

claims.statement: The GLP-1 options market has converged on deep out-of-the-money originator calls at peak hype pricing while the picks-and-shovels CDMO manufacturing layer — where the corpus locates the durable structural exposure — has no US derivatives expression; the decision packet verdict is watchlist_candidate because the expression vehicle does not yet exist in the accessible universe.
claims.falsification: Bachem or Polypeptide Group IR discloses order-book softening or capex deferral, indicating manufacturing constraint is resolving faster than the thesis assumes without the compensating originator supply signal. Lilly or Novo Nordisk announces vertical integration of peptide API capacity sufficient to materially reduce external CDMO dependence, collapsing the picks-and-shovels diversification thesis. FDA resolves the GLP-1 shortage in a way that collapses compounding-pharmacy volume without the originator supply growth that would validate the capacity-buildout thesis — breaking the shared-driver logic chain. CDMO names, once accessible via US-listed instruments, trade with sustained correlation to originator franchise risk, eliminating the diversification benefit the picks-and-shovels framing requires.
claims.object_title: Everyone Priced the Drug; No One Priced the Factory

claims.statement: Humanoid robotics has moved from demos into commercial pilots at BMW, Mercedes-Benz, Tesla, Symbotic, and Serve Robotics, but the options scanner returns one deep-OTM TSLA contract at MEDIUM convergence — the corpus is ahead of market pricing, the revaluation catalyst is Figure AI's financing or IPO timing, and that trigger has not moved.
claims.falsification: Figure AI postpones financing catalyst past Q2 2026 while BMW deployment remains a non-revenue pilot — watchlist conviction degrades, sector drops from primary candidates. Tesla removes Optimus production milestones from quarterly disclosure or shelves production beyond internal-use scope through 2027. Harmonic-drive, actuator, or rare-earth magnet suppliers disclose broad lead-time normalization while humanoid developers stop citing component cost or supply as a scaling constraint. BMW, Mercedes-Benz, or another top-ten auto OEM cancels a humanoid pilot citing unit economics, safety acceptance, or reliability as the primary reason.
claims.object_title: The Robot Has Left the Lab; the Market Has Not

claims.statement: The sector corpus now has 1 decision packets and 1 theses pointing at bitcoin mining, cicadas, and synthetic biology, while the scanner highlights MARA and the paper book already tracks 7 open and 59 closed rows.
claims.falsification: Reject the setup if packet invalidations fire, scanner asymmetry disappears, or the book exposure already prices the obvious expression.

claims.object_title: peptides: The Scanner Versus the Corpus

claims.statement: The options market has absorbed AI infrastructure through NVIDIA at peak-hype convergence while leaving TSMC — the CoWoS packaging node where BIS export-control compounding and HBM supply constraints converge — at minimum scanner attention, with the sector's strongest-sourced corpus claim on the primary risk variable and convergence at 0.25 on the equity.
claims.falsification: BIS materially relaxes advanced-computing or HBM controls for China, or major accelerator and memory suppliers report no China-control impact for two consecutive reporting periods — decision packet invalidation condition met. TSMC discloses HBM or advanced-packaging lead times falling below normal procurement windows while gross margin guidance normalizes, indicating bottleneck relief rather than continuing tightness. Alternative advanced packaging capacity from Samsung or SK Hynix achieves yield and volume parity with TSMC CoWoS for current-generation accelerator designs, reducing TSMC's chokepoint position structurally. Hyperscaler cluster architectures shift materially toward custom silicon or internal networking that reduces CoWoS attach rates, severing the capex-pull-through-to-foundry chain.
claims.object_title: The Factory the Chip Trade Forgot

claims.statement: AI infrastructure is the loudest thesis in public markets and the corpus confirms the underlying bottleneck dynamics are real — HBM and advanced packaging constraint, China export-control compounding risk, and hyperscaler capex pull-through visible in secondary-layer vendor IR — but the options scanner shows peak-hype convergence at the headline names and no clean asymmetric expression in the secondary bottleneck-stack names where the information edge actually lives. The decision packet correctly calls this a watchlist, not a trade.
claims.falsification: Microsoft, Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, and Oracle each guide AI infrastructure capex lower for two consecutive reporting cycles while NVDA, AVGO, DELL, ANET, and VRT all report decelerating AI backlog or order commentary — collapses the capex pull-through claim. Micron, Samsung, SK Hynix, or TSMC disclose HBM or advanced-packaging lead times falling below normal procurement windows while gross margin guidance normalizes — deflates the packaging bottleneck thesis. BIS materially relaxes advanced-computing or HBM controls for China, or major accelerator and memory suppliers report no China-control impact for two consecutive reporting periods — removes the export-control compounding claim. Hyperscaler cluster architectures shift away from the public-vendor bottleneck stack, with custom silicon, internal networking, or alternative accelerators reducing attach rates for NVDA, AVGO, ANET, MU, or VRT.
claims.object_title: All the Bottlenecks, None of the Asymmetry

claims.statement: Bitcoin mining has split into two structurally distinct businesses with different risk clocks — pure-play hashprice names (MARA, RIOT, CLSK) cooling post-peak and AI/HPC infrastructure platforms (CORZ, HUT, IREN, BTBT, HIVE) building lease duration quietly — while the corpus carries 0.80–0.83 confidence on three independent claims and the decision packet sits at watchlist_candidate awaiting a confirming PPA or earnings catalyst before equity entry is warranted.
claims.falsification: Hashprice stays below $40/PH/day for 4 consecutive weeks, compressing pure-play margins past cash-cost survivability. Bitmain ASIC landed costs spike 50% or more for 2 consecutive quarters, breaking fleet-upgrade economics for U.S. miners. Hyperscaler PPA or lease cancellations cluster across CORZ, HUT, IREN, BTBT, HIVE, WULF, or CIFR, signaling the AI pivot is not delivering promised margin. BTC sustains a sub-$30,000 drawdown past 12 months, collapsing sector cash-flow durability and BTC treasury optionality. Texas ERCOT rules or state mining restrictions force repeated curtailment or delayed interconnection for 2 consecutive quarters at major public-miner facilities.
claims.object_title: The Clocks Are Not Running at the Same Speed

claims.statement: DTC telehealth platforms have built material compounded GLP-1 revenues explicitly contingent on FDA shortage-list status — a binary regulatory event that collapses this revenue with no appeal mechanism. The scanner prices only the originator upside (LLY peak-hype convergence 0.80, NVO 0.68) while the platform cliff at the compounding layer goes unpriced: HIMS returns zero scanner contracts. As peptide-API manufacturing capacity grows, the probability of shortage resolution rises, pulling the regulatory clock forward on the same horizon as the CDMO picks-and-shovels thesis. The loud trade — originator calls — is fully taxed. The unpriced event risk sits one layer down.
claims.falsification: FDA extends shortage-list status indefinitely due to persistent demand outpacing supply — regulatory clock recedes rather than advances. HIMS materially diversifies revenue away from compounded GLP-1 before shortage resolution, replacing it with brand-name drug access or other therapeutic categories that reach scale. Originator pharma vertically integrates compounding-adjacent distribution channels, converting shortage resolution from a cliff to a managed commercial transition. Capacity announcements indicate material oversupply within two quarters but FDA shortage status remains — supply constraint and regulatory designation decouple, invalidating the shared-driver thesis.
claims.object_title: When the Shortage List Gets Resolved

claims.statement: U.S. public bitcoin mining has split into two structurally distinct businesses — pure-play hashprice beta (MARA, RIOT, CLSK) and AI/HPC power-infrastructure platforms (CORZ, HUT, IREN, BTBT, HIVE, WULF, BITF, CIFR) — at 0.82 corpus confidence sourced from Q1-Q2 2026 public miner filings, while the options scanner covers only the hashprice half at post_peak convergence and returns zero contracts for the infrastructure-pivot cohort. The pricing gap is structural: the market has built a derivatives surface for the BTC-cycle story and nothing for the hyperscaler-PPA rerating story.
claims.falsification: Hyperscaler AI/HPC lease or PPA cancellations cluster across two or more of Core Scientific, Hut 8, IREN, TeraWulf, Cipher, or Bit Digital in a single quarter, signaling the infrastructure pivot is not delivering contracted margin and collapsing the bifurcation thesis. Hashprice holds below $40 per PH per day for four consecutive weeks, pressing pure-play miner cash-cost survivability and reframing the sector as distress rather than bifurcation. Bitmain ASIC tariffs spike landed cost more than 50% for two consecutive quarters, breaking fleet-upgrade economics for ASIC-dependent pure-plays before the efficiency transition completes. BTC sustains a sub-$30,000 drawdown beyond 12 months, eliminating the BTC treasury optionality that keeps pure-play balance sheets solvent and compressing sector cash flow across both cohorts. Texas ERCOT large-load rules or state-level restrictions force repeated curtailment or delayed interconnection at major public-miner facilities for two consecutive quarters.
claims.object_title: Petahash Was the Cover Story

claims.statement: CRISPR Therapeutics has cleared the first commercial-compounding threshold in public gene editing — $43M Q1 2026 Casgevy revenue, 500-plus patient initiations, $2.44B cash — while the scanner returns one passing contract across the entire synthetic biology theme and equities have not re-rated from clinical-stage to commercial-compounder multiples. Simultaneously, the 2026-2027 engineered-biology cohort presents the densest independent multi-program validation calendar the sector has ever run, across base editing, prime editing, ex-vivo CRISPR, in-vivo delivery, and AI-designed therapeutics — the first year the platform question gets tested across programs rather than answered one program at a time.
claims.falsification: Casgevy patient-initiation count declines for two consecutive quarters or Vertex discloses formal payer-reimbursement headwinds in post-market filings, breaking the commercial-compounding anchor that justifies the CRSP revaluation thesis. Two or more named clinical or regulatory events — Beam BEAM-302 pivotal cohort initiation, Intellia lonvo-z BLA or nex-z MAGNITUDE resumption, Prime Medicine PM359/PM577/PM647, Recursion REC-1245/REC-4881/REC-4539, Takeda zasocitinib NDA, Lilly VERVE-102 post-close update — miss, delay beyond 2027, or halt on safety grounds, collapsing the platform-validation case. FDA CBER issues guidance materially tightening BLA, IND, or post-market surveillance requirements for somatic gene-editing products, raising the regulatory premium for Casgevy while reducing addressable market for the pre-commercial cohort. Twist Bioscience cuts fiscal-2026 revenue guidance or Ginkgo Bioworks misses 2026 cash-burn guidance or pushes adjusted-EBITDA breakeven beyond 2027, eliminating the foundry-and-supply margin argument. A mega-cap acquirer takes a CRISPR or prime-editing pure play out at less than 25% premium to its 30-day VWAP, signaling that strategic value tops out below standalone platform expectations.
claims.object_title: Five Hundred Patients Before the Market Noticed

claims.statement: Peptide API manufacturing capacity is the structural constraint on GLP-1 supply — observable in primary CDMO investor filings at 0.80 corpus confidence — while originator names trade at peak-hype convergence and the manufacturing layer sits entirely outside option flow. The picks-and-shovels CDMO expression carries lower franchise-headline risk and has not been priced by the narrative wave that has taxed LLY and NVO.
claims.falsification: Capacity announcements indicate material oversupply within two quarters and CDMO order books soften on guidance calls Lilly or Novo Nordisk announces vertical integration into peptide-API capacity, reducing external CDMO dependence FDA enforcement removes compounded-GLP-1 from the market in a way that collapses downstream telehealth volume without a compensating originator-supply shock Bachem and PolyPeptide Group begin trading with sustained correlation to LLY/NVO franchise headlines, eliminating the picks-and-shovels diversification premise
claims.object_title: The Factory Behind the Drug Nobody Can Get

claims.statement: Humanoid robotics has crossed from demo to commercial pilot — Tesla/Optimus in quarterly IR, Figure/BMW under formal agreement, Apptronik at Mercedes-Benz — while the options market prices TSLA at MEDIUM/growing convergence (0.50) with no isolation of the actuator supply constraint that governs cost-per-unit and deployment velocity. The Figure AI financing catalyst is the public repricing event that has not been timed.
claims.falsification: Figure AI postpones an IPO or primary financing catalyst past Q2 2026 while BMW deployment remains limited to non-revenue pilots — removes the private-to-public catalyst clock. Tesla shelves Optimus production beyond internal-use scope through 2027 or removes Optimus production milestones from quarterly disclosure — removes the highest-visibility public data point. Harmonic-drive, actuator, and rare-earth magnet suppliers disclose broad lead-time normalization while humanoid developers stop citing component cost or supply as a scaling constraint — actuator bottleneck thesis is wrong. BMW, Mercedes-Benz, or another top-ten auto OEM cancels a humanoid pilot citing unit economics, safety acceptance, or reliability as the primary reason — demand-side invalidation.
claims.object_title: The Gearbox Between Demo and Revenue

claims.statement: The GLP-1 obesity trade is fully priced at the originator level — LLY and NVO sit at peak-hype convergence with elevated IV and deep-OTM options expressing a narrative the market already knows. The corpus thesis is that the structurally durable exposure is upstream: specialized peptide-API CDMO capacity, where the supply bottleneck is observable in primary IR and the names carry lower franchise-headline correlation than the originators. Compounding-pharmacy channels are a contingent second-order beneficiary whose value depends on FDA shortage-status as a binary switch.
claims.falsification: Capacity announcements indicate material oversupply within two quarters and CDMO order books soften — removes the supply-constraint underpinning of the picks-and-shovels thesis. Originator pharma vertically integrates peptide-API capacity and reduces external CDMO dependence — eliminates the structural advantage of holding CDMO names. FDA enforcement removes compounded GLP-1 from the market without a compensating originator-supply shock — collapses the downstream volume signal without creating CDMO upside. CDMO names begin trading with sustained originator-pharma franchise correlation — eliminates the diversification benefit that makes the picks-and-shovels reframe distinct from simply owning LLY.
claims.object_title: The Market Is Long the Brand, Short the Molecule

claims.statement: Photonic computing has cleared three simultaneous conditions for a quiet setup: NVIDIA-anchored revenue inflection at 0.88 corpus confidence, private chiplet validation at multi-billion-dollar marks, and zero passing options contracts on LITE or COHR. The scanner's loudest signals sit in peak-hype names where the narrative has fully taxed the trade. The cross-sector pattern — private marks leading public options pricing by 12-24 months — is documented consistently across synthetic biology, defense AI, longevity, and edge AI. The asymmetric position today is in the sectors where corpus confidence is highest and scanner coverage is absent.
claims.falsification: Primary: NVIDIA restructures or reduces the $4B LITE/COHR commitment in any 8-K, 10-Q, or earnings-call disclosure — this removes the customer anchor the revaluation rests on and breaks the thesis. Secondary: LITE or COHR misses hyperscaler optical revenue or OCS backlog conversion by more than 25% in one quarter, breaking the revenue-inflection case. Tertiary: a non-photonic interconnect alternative wins material design-in slots away from LITE/COHR/Lightmatter/Ayar at OFC 2026-2027 equivalent events. Cross-sector validity check: if CRSP Casgevy patient-initiation count declines for two consecutive quarters, the foundational claim that corpus-high/scanner-dark sectors have durable asymmetry loses its primary validated instance.
claims.object_title: The Optical Layer Running Ahead of the Tape

claims.statement: Photonic computing has crossed a confirmed revenue inflection — Lumentum +90% YoY, Coherent +21% YoY, NVIDIA $4B structural anchor — while the scanner records zero options contracts on both primary names and the convergence scorer still classifies the theme as emerging; the same AI-capex supercycle is visible in nuclear-SMR and defense-AI, but options premiums in those sectors have already partially taxed the expression.
claims.falsification: NVIDIA, Lumentum, or Coherent publicly reduces or restructures the March 2, 2026 $4B commitment in a newsroom release, 8-K, 10-Q, or earnings-call disclosure — this is the single biggest load-bearing support for the photonic revaluation. LITE or COHR misses guidance on hyperscaler optical revenue or OCS backlog conversion by more than 25% in any subsequent quarter, eliminating the revenue-inflection case. A non-photonic interconnect alternative (advanced copper, in-package electrical fabric, or hyperscaler-internal optical engines) wins material design-in slots away from LITE or COHR at OFC 2027 or an equivalent industry disclosure event. Anduril postpones an IPO or primary financing event past end-2026 with no replacement private valuation mark, breaking the defense-AI private-mark catalyst clock the public adjacents revalue against. Hyperscaler nuclear PPA flow falls below 1 GW of newly announced contracted or development-stage capacity for two consecutive quarters, removing demand-visibility anchor for OKLO and SMR.
claims.object_title: Revenue Is Moving in the Dark

claims.statement: Silicon photonics is generating the real revenue inflection inside AI infrastructure while options flow concentrates in peak-hype names one layer up the stack. NVIDIA's $4B anchor commitment in March 2026, combined with 90% and 21% year-over-year revenue growth at Lumentum and Coherent respectively, and a validated private chiplet layer (Lightmatter, Ayar Labs, Marvell-Celestial), constitutes the strongest customer-anchored demand signal in any AI hardware subsector. The scanner found zero contracts against the photonic computing theme. LITE carries a convergence score of 0.4 while NVDA sits at 0.8. The market is pricing the AI buildout through the GPU and networking layer and has not yet routed flow into the optics bottleneck beneath it.
claims.falsification: NVIDIA, Lumentum, or Coherent publicly reduces or restructures the March 2 2026 $4B commitment in a newsroom release, 8-K, 10-Q, or earnings-call disclosure — this is the single load-bearing demand anchor for the thesis LITE or COHR misses one quarter of hyperscaler optical revenue or OCS backlog conversion by more than 25%, eliminating the revenue-inflection case A non-photonic interconnect alternative — advanced copper, in-package electrical fabric, or hyperscaler-built optical engines — wins material design-in slots at the 2026-2027 OFC equivalent away from LITE, COHR, AAOI, Lightmatter, or Ayar TSMC reports in earnings materials that CoWoS-class advanced-packaging capacity is no longer tight for AI/HPC customers, removing the supply-ramp pricing-power premise for co-packaged optics Scanner coverage gap confirmed: LITE and COHR options chains fail to generate any qualifying contracts even after expanded sweep parameters, indicating the options market is genuinely absent and the thesis must be expressed in equity not derivatives
claims.object_title: The Light Nobody Is Trading

claims.statement: Optical interconnect has crossed from research to production revenue inside the hyperscaler AI buildout, anchored by NVIDIA's March 2026 $4B dual commitment to Lumentum and Coherent, yet the options scanner finds zero qualifying structures for either name while private chiplet marks at Lightmatter ($4.4B) and Ayar Labs ($3.75B) signal the same 12-to-24-month private-leads-public revaluation pattern visible across five other sectors in this corpus.
claims.falsification: NVIDIA, Lumentum, or Coherent publicly reduces or restructures the March 2 2026 $4B commitment in an 8-K, 10-Q, or earnings-call disclosure LITE or COHR misses one quarter of hyperscaler optical revenue or OCS backlog conversion guidance by more than 25% A non-photonic interconnect alternative — advanced copper, in-package electrical fabric, or internally built hyperscaler optical engines — wins material design-in slots at a 2026-2027 OFC equivalent TSMC or a named ecosystem partner discloses a slip in COUPE or CoWoS-class silicon-photonic CPO qualification milestones
claims.object_title: The Bandwidth Wall Is Already Photons
